identity.name: Linux Lateral Movement — SSH Abuse, Tunneling and Internal Recon (T1021.004)
identity.summary: A web service account runs internal recon (interfaces, routes, ARP), port-scans via bash /dev/tcp, attempts batch-mode SSH, builds SSH local-forward and SOCKS tunnels, then enumerates DNS and host files. Cortex XDR for Linux detects the scanning, SSH abuse and tunneling via command-line BIOCs and stitches the chain into one lateral-movement incident.
identity.description: Once on a Linux host, attackers map the internal network and reuse SSH to spread. Unit 42's Linux threat landscape reporting highlights SSH abuse and tunneling as the dominant lateral-movement pattern on servers. This scenario reproduces the full chain from a non-interactive service account: network reconnaissance with ip addr/route/neigh, a bash /dev/tcp port sweep across common service ports, batch-mode SSH (BatchMode=yes, StrictHostKeyChecking=no) to discovered hosts, SSH tunneling with -L local forward and -D SOCKS proxy, and remote-system discovery via /etc/hosts, resolv.conf and DNS lookups. The detection approach is command-line behavioral analysis: the /dev/tcp sweep, batch-mode SSH, and tunnel flags each map to a Cortex XDR BIOC, while analytics layers recognize a service account performing host/network discovery and automated SSH. A correlation rule fuses the recon, scan, SSH and tunnel activity into a single lateral-movement incident — all connections in the lab target localhost so the simulation is safe.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1021.004 / T1046 / T1572 — Linux SSH lateral-movement and tunneling atomic tests

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the target.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-EDR-005 requirements (ssh client and ip/iproute2 available).

payload.code:
# step-1: network recon
ip addr show; ip route show; ip neigh show
# step-2: port sweep via /dev/tcp (localhost for safety)
for port in 22 80 443 3306 5432 8080 8888; do timeout 1 bash -c "echo >/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/$port" 2>/dev/null && echo "localhost:$port OPEN"; done
# step-3: batch-mode SSH
timeout 5 ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=3 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no www-data@127.0.0.1 'whoami' 2>&1
# step-4: SSH tunnels
timeout 5 ssh -o ConnectTimeout=3 -N -L 8443:127.0.0.1:443 localhost & sleep 2; timeout 5 ssh -o ConnectTimeout=3 -N -D 1080 localhost & sleep 2
# step-5: remote system discovery
hostname -f; cat /etc/hosts; cat /etc/resolv.conf

expected_artifacts.description: ip addr/route/neigh enumeration from a service account.
bash /dev/tcp port sweep across multiple ports.
ssh invoked with BatchMode=yes and StrictHostKeyChecking=no.
ssh invoked with -L or -D tunneling flags.
Host/DNS enumeration via /etc/hosts, resolv.conf and nslookup/dig.
Outbound TCP connections to SSH/service ports during the sweep and SSH attempts.

biocs.name: EDR-005 Port Scan Via Bash devtcp
biocs.description: Port scanning via bash /dev/tcp — network service discovery from web service account. A bash loop probes multiple ports through /dev/tcp.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/dev/tcp/"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "node", "postgres", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: EDR-005 Batch Mode SSH From Service Account
biocs.description: SSH connection attempt from service account — lateral movement via remote service. ssh runs with BatchMode=yes and StrictHostKeyChecking=no under a service identity.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "ssh"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("BatchMode=yes", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "node", "postgres", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: EDR-005 SSH Tunnel Creation With Forward Flags
biocs.description: SSH tunnel creation (-L/-D flags) — protocol tunneling for covert channel establishment. ssh is invoked with -L local forward, -D SOCKS, or -R remote forward.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "ssh"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (" -L ", " -D ", " -R ", " -w ")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains " -N"

xql_queries.name: EDR-005 Analytics Network Interface And Routing Enumeration
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("ip", "ifconfig", "route", "arp")
| filter actor_effective_username != "root"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-005 Analytics Sequential Port Connection Attempts
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh") and action_process_image_command_line contains "/dev/tcp/"
| comp count() as probe_attempts by agent_hostname, causality_actor_process_instance_id
| filter probe_attempts >= 3

xql_queries.name: EDR-005 Analytics Batch SSH StrictHostKey Disabled
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "ssh"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "StrictHostKeyChecking=no"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-005 Analytics SSH Port Forwarding From Service Account
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "ssh"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (" -L ", " -D ", " -R ")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-005 Analytics DNS And Host File Enumeration
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/etc/hosts", "resolv.conf", "nslookup", "dig", "getent hosts", "hostname -f")
| filter actor_effective_username != "root"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

correlation_rules.name: Linux Lateral Movement Chain — Recon, Scan, SSH, Tunnel
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(EDR-005 Port Scan Via Bash devtcp) OR BIOC(EDR-005 Batch Mode SSH From Service Account) OR BIOC(EDR-005 SSH Tunnel Creation With Forward Flags)

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR Analytics — Service account performing internal network discovery
Cortex XDR Analytics — Automated SSH lateral movement

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name = "ssh" and action_process_image_command_line contains_any (" -L ", " -D ") | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Correlation CR-EDR-0005 fuses recon, port sweep, batch-mode SSH and tunneling into one lateral-movement incident.
Playbook isolates the source host, kills SSH tunnels and revokes the service account's SSH keys.

use_cases.name: Lateral Movement Detection — Linux SSH and Tunneling
use_cases.description: Detect internal recon, /dev/tcp port scanning, batch-mode SSH abuse and SSH tunneling from a service account and stitch them into one incident.

test_cases.objective: The scan, SSH and tunnel behaviors fire their corresponding Cortex XDR BIOCs.
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 installed and healthy.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe BIOC alerts for port scan, batch SSH and tunneling in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `EDR-005 Port Scan Via Bash devtcp` fires within 60s.
BIOC `EDR-005 SSH Tunnel Creation With Forward Flags` fires within 60s.
All five XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Lateral-movement chain stitches into one incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-EDR-0005` groups recon, scan, SSH and tunnel activity on one host into a single incident.
Incident is attributed to the originating service account.

preventive_controls: Deny SSH client use by service accounts; restrict outbound SSH with a default-deny egress policy.
Segment internal networks so a single foothold cannot scan or reach broad service ports.
Disable SSH port forwarding (AllowTcpForwarding no) where not required.
Require key-with-certificate SSH auth and rotate service-account keys regularly.
detection_engineering: Alert on /dev/tcp loops, on ssh BatchMode=yes/StrictHostKeyChecking=no, and on ssh -L/-D/-R/-N from service accounts.
Correlate internal recon (ip/route/neigh, /etc/hosts, resolv.conf) with subsequent SSH attempts.
Flag short-interval connections to many internal ports as service discovery.
